jokes.text: I'm gonna get 12 eggs and part of a dead animal. Dealer's choice. Please and thank you.
jokes.setup: Normal restaurant ordering scene
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt, primal description of breakfast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character moment - could only come from him. The 'dealer's choice' politeness contrasts beautifully with the caveman description.

jokes.text: I will have catfish and grits, with pumpernickel toast.
jokes.setup: Ann orders something completely unlike her usual healthy choices
jokes.punchline: The bizarre combination sets up her explanation
jokes.explanation: Setup for Ann's dating-herself concept. Functional but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Now I have two best friends, Ann and Ann. Each one more beautiful than the other.
jokes.setup: Ann explains she's dating herself
jokes.punchline: Leslie's enthusiastic endorsement of Ann talking to herself
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie - taking Ann's concept and making it even more over-the-top. The 'each one more beautiful' is perfect Leslie hyperbole.

jokes.text: Which Ann is screaming like a maniac? Oatmeal Ann or catfish Ann? I'm not sure, because I instantly blacked out from extreme terror.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about skydiving photo using Ann's two-personality concept
jokes.punchline: Ann admits she blacked out immediately from fear
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the two-Ann concept while revealing Ann's cowardice. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You know what? I probably shouldn't come. I'll embarrass the both of you.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about being good at ice skating
jokes.punchline: Leslie's concern about embarrassing both Anns
jokes.explanation: Leslie playing into Ann's concept. Character-appropriate but mild impact.

jokes.text: But you are so brilliant and kind and stupid hot.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving supportive speech to Ann
jokes.punchline: The casual 'stupid hot' insertion
jokes.explanation: Pure Leslie - mixing sincere compliments with her characteristic bluntness about Ann's attractiveness.

jokes.text: If she were dating a guy for six weeks, and they decided to have a kid, I'd be like, 'Congratulations, Ann... And Channing Tatum.' Because that is the only scenario that would make sense to me.
jokes.setup: Leslie's talking head about Ann dating herself for six weeks
jokes.punchline: Only Channing Tatum would justify such a quick decision
jokes.explanation: Great structure with the pause before revealing Channing Tatum. Perfect Leslie logic - only a movie star would justify this timeline.

jokes.text: What a gorgeous herbaceous medley. There's been a mistake. You've accidentally given me the food that my food eats.
jokes.setup: Ron is served a salad
jokes.punchline: His polite but devastating critique
jokes.explanation: Iconic Ron line. Perfect economy, character integration, and the politeness makes it even funnier. 'Food that my food eats' is brilliantly quotable.

jokes.text: Is a gerbil marrying a rabbit?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions salad is traditional first course at weddings
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and classic Ron logic. The specificity of gerbil and rabbit makes it funnier than generic 'animals.'

jokes.text: Tom considers himself a foodie, which apparently means taking instagrams of food instead of eating it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains the panel setup
jokes.punchline: Her dig at Tom's social media habits
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about modern food culture. Good character moment for both Leslie and Tom.

jokes.text: Number two's was subtle and provocative, like a coy Dutch woman guarding a dark secret.
jokes.setup: Tom is supposed to evaluate catering
jokes.punchline: His pretentious, nonsensical food description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - overly elaborate metaphor that means nothing. 'Coy Dutch woman guarding a dark secret' is wonderfully specific absurdity.

jokes.text: Number three's told a story-- a story from a book I wouldn't read but I would watch the movie of.
jokes.setup: Tom continues his pretentious food reviews
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't read books
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Tom trying to sound sophisticated while revealing his shallow nature.

jokes.text: Delicate little dough pocket filled with tomato sauce, cheese, and seasoned meat. Just a stunning culinary innovation. It was a calzone. It was literally just a small calzone.
jokes.setup: Ron describes the appetizer in flowery terms
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man delivery from Ben. The elaborate description followed by 'literally just a small calzone' is great structure.

jokes.text: No. Tragically, we are both heterosexual.
jokes.setup: Sperm bank worker asks if Leslie and Ann are a couple
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic response about their sexual orientations
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overreaction. 'Tragically' sells the joke - she genuinely seems disappointed they can't be together.

jokes.text: Some sperm. I meant in terms of a donor? Oh, you know, nice guy... Nice sperm.
jokes.setup: Worker asks what Ann is looking for
jokes.punchline: Ann's awkward, literal response
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe comedy. Ann's awkwardness in an awkward situation. Functional but not standout.

jokes.text: So did the Unabomber... Yeah, so did Lance Armstrong, and he turned out to be a lying drug user. Or a hero. I don't know where we stand on him now.
jokes.setup: Ann points out positive donor qualities
jokes.punchline: Leslie's paranoid counterexamples and confusion about Lance Armstrong
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and the Lance Armstrong confusion is a nice topical touch. Leslie's paranoia drives the humor.

jokes.text: Whoa, Sewage Joe... He is the gentleman who was fired for emailing a picture of his penis to every woman in city hall. Guilty. Yeah, that's what the judge said.
jokes.setup: Joe appears unexpectedly at sperm bank
jokes.punchline: Joe's proud admission and reference to legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Callback to established character. Joe's shameless pride in his legal troubles is appropriately gross.

jokes.text: Free money, free porn. Best job I ever had. Also... If you're looking to buy some weed, I'm looking as well.
jokes.setup: Joe explains why he donates sperm
jokes.punchline: His casual drug solicitation
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross for the character but relies on shock value. The casual weed request is a good character touch.

jokes.text: You realize you've said that about all of them? Well, she has, every single time. 'Cause she is super hot. Honestly, you'd look hot naked.
jokes.setup: Ben says Leslie looks hot in her outfit
jokes.punchline: Andy's increasingly enthusiastic agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character moments - Ben's supportive boyfriend behavior and Andy's inappropriate enthusiasm.

jokes.text: This suit makes me want to scold a Catholic child.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying on Ann Taylor suit
jokes.punchline: Her specific, weird reaction to the conservative styling
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unexpected. The image is perfectly weird and captures how conservative clothing affects Leslie's personality.

jokes.text: I don't know who Ann Taylor is, but I hate her and I want to kill her.
jokes.setup: Leslie's discomfort with conservative clothing continues
jokes.punchline: Her violent reaction to the designer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the previous line. Leslie's intense reaction to clothing brands is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: She had the patch sewn in. You can't remove it. What's in the pocket, you ask? A ticket stub from a Fleetwood Mac concert in 1995
jokes.setup: Ben explains Leslie's old pantsuit
jokes.punchline: The specific concert memorabilia permanently sewn in
jokes.explanation: Great character detail. The specificity of Fleetwood Mac 1995 and the fact it's sewn in shows Leslie's attachment to memories.

jokes.text: So tomorrow, I lead a public forum in Leslie's Fleetwood Mac sex pants.
jokes.setup: April reluctantly accepts she has to wear Leslie's suit
jokes.punchline: Her blunt, inappropriate description
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery. 'Fleetwood Mac sex pants' is brilliantly quotable and captures April's inappropriate directness.

jokes.text: Fleetwood Mac Sex Pants. New band name. I call it. Ooh, you know what? Maybe just Fleetwood Mac.
jokes.setup: April says 'Fleetwood Mac sex pants'
jokes.punchline: Andy's band name excitement and then realizing Fleetwood Mac exists
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his enthusiasm for band names and complete obliviousness to existing famous bands.

jokes.text: Sperm that is worthy of your perfect eggs does not grow on trees.
jokes.setup: Leslie advises Ann about finding quality sperm donors
jokes.punchline: The mixed metaphor about sperm growing on trees
jokes.explanation: Leslie's characteristic over-the-top support mixed with an absurd biological metaphor. Good economy.

jokes.text: his favorite season of Friends, whether or not he hates jazz-- hopefully, he does.
jokes.setup: Leslie lists important things to know about potential fathers
jokes.punchline: Her specific opinion about jazz hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character specificity. Leslie's random strong opinions (anti-jazz) mixed with relationship basics.

jokes.text: In this case, the body is Ann's, and the woman in charge of it is me.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about women being in charge of their own bodies
jokes.punchline: Admits she's actually in charge of Ann's decisions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Leslie's complete lack of self-awareness about her controlling nature, delivered with perfect conviction.

jokes.text: like Knuckle-hair Dave from HR. I don't mind the knuckle hair. I just think he's a dick.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions eliminating Knuckle-hair Dave
jokes.punchline: Ann's clarification about why he's eliminated
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation it's about the knuckle hair, but it's his personality. Ann's bluntness is funny.

jokes.text: Small office. Mine's way bigger... The seats in my office are leather.
jokes.setup: Dr. Harris sits down for interview
jokes.punchline: His immediate insecure bragging
jokes.explanation: Standard insecure male behavior. Creates appropriate cringe but nothing special.

jokes.text: Guy's got a spinal fracture. He's not going anywhere.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggests he check on his patient
jokes.punchline: His callous assessment of immobilized patient
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals his poor bedside manner. Good economy and appropriate for the character elimination.

jokes.text: I'm dying. I was dying earlier today. And then I died. Now I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Chris appears sick with food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His dramatic timeline of death progression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris over-dramatization. The progression from dying to dead is classically Chris-like hyperbole.

jokes.text: I cracked the bottom of the toilet bowl.
jokes.setup: Ron describes his food poisoning symptoms
jokes.punchline: The extreme physical damage he caused
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross and specific to Ron's intense physicality. Great economy - says everything about the severity in few words.

jokes.text: I have voided more than Tom's body weight in the last 12 hours alone. He might have just disappeared off the earth.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Tom must be feeling
jokes.punchline: Ron's mathematical assessment of bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - treating bodily functions with mathematical precision. The Tom body weight comparison is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: As Eleanor Roosevelt once said to Betty Ford... 'Hillary Clinton is great.'
jokes.setup: April attempts to channel Leslie's inspirational quotes
jokes.punchline: Her completely nonsensical historical attribution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment. The timeline makes no sense and the quote is generic, perfectly capturing her half-hearted attempt to be Leslie.

jokes.text: Friendship bands that I've made for each and every one of you. So now, I guess we're Park Pals.
jokes.setup: April tries to recreate Leslie's enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Her deadpan delivery of cheerful concepts
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - attempting Leslie's friendship initiatives with zero enthusiasm. The phrase 'Park Pals' delivered deadpan is perfect.

jokes.text: You should see the booger under this chair.
jokes.setup: April's friendship bands surprise
jokes.punchline: Citizen's gross discovery
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that doesn't add much. Functional for showing the venue's problems but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I see what's going on here. You want my sperm. When will women in this town stop scheming to get my sperm?
jokes.setup: Pete realizes what the interview is about
jokes.punchline: His assumption this happens frequently
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor. The idea that multiple women are constantly trying to get his sperm suggests a delusional self-image.

jokes.text: That son of a bitch is astute.
jokes.setup: Pete correctly identifies the sperm scheme
jokes.punchline: Leslie's grudging respect for his insight
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie reaction. Her surprise that someone saw through her scheme, mixed with professional respect for his awareness.

jokes.text: Oh, whoa! Smells like some vomit took a dump in here.
jokes.setup: Tom enters the office where everyone has food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His grossly specific description of the smell
jokes.explanation: Tom's characteristic crude metaphor. 'Vomit took a dump' is appropriately gross and specific to his style.

jokes.text: I didn't eat those stupid mini-calzones. Haver-food rule number six-- never eat anything with a sauce I have to dip myself. Drizzle it on for me. I'm not your maid.
jokes.setup: Tom explains why he's not sick
jokes.punchline: His pretentious food rules and demanding attitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom - his pretentious food rules and entitlement. The 'I'm not your maid' is peak Tom self-importance.

jokes.text: The calzones... betrayed me? Never again, guys. As God as my witness, they're dead to me.
jokes.setup: Ben realizes calzones caused the food poisoning
jokes.punchline: His dramatic Gone with the Wind reference about calzones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to Ben's calzone obsession. The Gone with the Wind reference elevates his heartbreak to epic proportions. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Is this gonna be a topless park? I don't think there are topless parks. Well, let's build the first one and be heroes.
jokes.setup: Harris asks about topless park
jokes.punchline: His heroic vision for topless park innovation
jokes.explanation: Harris being predictably creepy but the 'heroes' angle adds a funny layer of self-importance to his perversion.

jokes.text: Topless park! Topless park!
jokes.setup: Harris promotes his topless park idea
jokes.punchline: The crowd enthusiastically chanting for it
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation showing how bad ideas can gain momentum, but relies on crude humor without much wit.

jokes.text: Actually, a farting immigrant segment would be really funny. Ay dios... Good looking out, Leslie.
jokes.setup: Leslie sarcastically suggests racist radio content
jokes.punchline: Howard genuinely considers it good and tries it out
jokes.explanation: Shows Howard's poor judgment and casual racism. Creates appropriate cringe but uncomfortable subject matter.

jokes.text: I'm gonna scan a quick pic of them boobies for a little deposit in 'El banco de spanko.'
jokes.setup: Howard being inappropriate as usual
jokes.punchline: His gross Spanish nickname for masturbation
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross for the Douche character but relies heavily on crude shock value. Low craft but serves character purpose.

jokes.text: Well, frankly, I don't think it's for you to decide.
jokes.setup: Leslie says Howard wouldn't be right for the job
jokes.punchline: Howard's surprisingly mature response
jokes.explanation: Surprising moment where the Douche shows genuine maturity and appropriate boundaries. Good misdirection from his usual behavior.

jokes.text: And if we had a boy, I don't know, I'd name him something funny, like 'Dick' or 'O. J.'
jokes.setup: Howard thoughtfully discusses potential children's names
jokes.punchline: His terrible name choices with dark implications
jokes.explanation: Shows Howard's poor judgment despite his momentary maturity. The O.J. reference adds dark humor to his obliviousness.

jokes.text: So, Crazy Ira, remember that chick Ann I used to... Yeah, she was smoking hot! Turns out, she wants to have my baby.
jokes.setup: Howard on the radio discussing Ann
jokes.punchline: His crude public discussion of private matters
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awful behavior that creates justified anger from Ann. Shows consequences of Leslie's interference.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. I just wanted to toss my name into the ring, in respect to this Indian woman's vagina.
jokes.setup: Milton appears unexpectedly in Ann's office
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate and racist volunteer offer
jokes.explanation: Creates appropriate disgust and shows how word spreads in Pawnee, but relies on crude shock value and uncomfortable racial elements.

jokes.text: 'Me so horny.' That was not me. That was a farcical sound effect.
jokes.setup: Leslie appears on Howard's radio show
jokes.punchline: Her panicked clarification about racist sound effect
jokes.explanation: Leslie's desperation to distance herself from offensive content. Shows her discomfort in the Douche's world.

jokes.text: The fartsicle. Frozen farts you can eat. Put it in your face-mouth! It's cold and it smells. God, how did you make that so fast?
jokes.setup: Leslie says 'farcical'
jokes.punchline: Howard's instant gross product concept
jokes.explanation: Shows Howard's quick wit for gross content. 'Face-mouth' is a funny touch but overall relies on gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Fine, I will stand there for one second and say it, and then we're done. Fair's fair. Somebody get me a pudding pop.
jokes.setup: Leslie agreeing to Jell-o wrestling terms
jokes.punchline: Her Bill Cosby reference while agreeing to the ridiculous demand
jokes.explanation: Leslie's desperate compromise with appropriate Cosby Show reference for the Rudy context.

jokes.text: Rudy. There, happy? No, say it like Bill Cosby. Rudy.
jokes.setup: Leslie agrees to stand in Jell-o and say 'Rudy'
jokes.punchline: Howard's demand for Cosby impression escalates the humiliation
jokes.explanation: Creates appropriate discomfort showing how Howard escalates demands. Leslie's desperation to help Ann drives the scene.

jokes.text: This bag is full of Fruit Roll-Ups and baseball cards.
jokes.setup: April needs her Leslie costume for the meeting
jokes.punchline: Andy's bag contains his personal snacks instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy mistake - the contents are perfectly childish for his character. Creates crisis for April's performance.

jokes.text: Look, you won't have to pay any extra money, okay? And don't ever call me 'ma'am' again.
jokes.setup: Citizen's rude interruption
jokes.punchline: April's blunt response and personal offense at 'ma'am'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April response showing her natural authority and age sensitivity.

jokes.text: Nobody wants your creepy stoner eyes staring at them while they're enjoying a park, okay? Stop being gross.
jokes.setup: Harris brings up topless park again
jokes.punchline: April's blunt shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - no patience for nonsense. 'Creepy stoner eyes' is specifically insulting and 'stop being gross' is perfectly direct.

jokes.text: Shut me down! You scare me, and I gotta say, I kinda dig that.
jokes.setup: April shuts down Harris aggressively
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate attraction to her hostility
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy response that shows April's effectiveness. Creates discomfort but serves the character dynamics.

jokes.text: Besides, what is more cuter-us than your uterus?
jokes.setup: Leslie makes a reproductive system pun
jokes.punchline: The terrible 'cuter-us/uterus' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun delivered with Leslie's characteristic enthusiasm. The badness of the pun is part of the charm.

jokes.text: Let's make a baby together. You should phrase that differently.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann head off to work on baby plans
jokes.punchline: Ann's realization of how it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good awareness moment. Leslie's obliviousness to how things sound contrasts with Ann's social awareness.

jokes.text: I wanted to bone her right there in that dirty church basement.
jokes.setup: Andy compliments April's performance
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate venue for sexual desire
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy inappropriate timing and venue. Shows his attraction to April's competence but crudely expressed.

jokes.text: Well, frankly, you should have thought of that before we burned it.
jokes.setup: Leslie wants her burned pantsuit back
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact response about the irreversible burning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - treating the burning as a foregone conclusion. The 'frankly' makes it even funnier, adding fake authority.
